relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The bronze altar is made square and strong.
Its horns rise from its corners.
Bronze vessels tend the altar.
A bronze network fits beneath the ledge.
Rings prepare the altar for movement.
Bronze-covered poles are made.
The altar can be carried through wilderness.
Women’s mirrors become the bronze basin of washing.
The court is shaped with linen hangings.
Bronze bases and silver hooks hold the south.
The north side mirrors the structure.
The west side closes the court.
The east side faces sunrise.
One gate shoulder is measured.
The second gate shoulder is measured.
Fine linen surrounds the court.
Bronze and silver hold the pillars.
The gate screen carries sacred colors.
The gate pillars stand in bronze and silver.
All pegs are bronze.
The dwelling’s materials are counted by Moses’ word and Levite service.
Bezalel made all YHWH commanded.
Oholiab worked as engraver, designer, and embroiderer.
The gold of holy work is counted.
The silver of the counted congregation is counted.
Each counted life gives the half-shekel.
Silver becomes bases for holy place and veil.
Remaining silver forms hooks, tops, and bands.
The bronze offering is counted.
Bronze forms bases, altar, grating, and vessels.
Bronze grounds the court and pegs.

contemplative_note: Exodus 38 makes accountability sacred: women’s mirrors become a basin of cleansing, every metal is counted, and equal life-silver holds up the dwelling.
